name: xmount
title: xmount
summary: xmount - Tool to crossmount between multiple input and output harddisk image files

commands.name: xmount
commands.description: Tool to crossmount between multiple input and output harddisk image files

arguments.description: Enable virtual write support.
<ifile> : Input image format and source file(s). May be specified multiple
arguments.raw: --in  <ifile> : Input image format and source file(s). May be specified multiple

arguments.description: Print out infos about used compiler and loaded libraries.
arguments.raw: --info  Print out infos about used compiler and loaded libraries.

arguments.description: Specify input library specific options.
: Morphing function to apply to input image(s). If not specified, defaults
arguments.raw: --morph  : Morphing function to apply to input image(s). If not specified, defaults

arguments.description: Specify morphing library specific options.
Move the output image data start <off> bytes into the input image(s).
arguments.raw: --offset  Move the output image data start <off> bytes into the input image(s).

arguments.description: Output image format. If not specified, defaults to "raw".
arguments.raw: --out  Output image format. If not specified, defaults to "raw".

arguments.description: Same as --cache <file> but overwrites existing cache file.
arguments.raw: --owcache  Same as --cache <file> but overwrites existing cache file.

arguments.description: Same as --cache <file> but does **not** allow further writes.
arguments.raw: --rocache  Same as --cache <file> but does **not** allow further writes.

arguments.description: <size> : The data end of input image(s) is set to no more than <size> bytes af-
arguments.raw: --sizelimit  <size> : The data end of input image(s) is set to no more than <size> bytes af-

arguments.description: Same as --info.

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# xmount -h
 
 xmount v1.2.1 Copyright (c) 2024-2025 by SITS Sarl <development@sits.lu>
 
 Usage:
 xmount [fopts] <xopts> <mntp>
 
 Options:
 fopts:
   -d : Enable FUSE's and xmount's debug mode.
   -h : Display this help message.
   -s : Run single threaded.
   -o no_allow_other : Disable automatic addition of FUSE's allow_other option.
   -o <fopts> : Specify fuse mount options. Will also disable automatic addition of FUSE's allow_other option!
 
 xopts:
   --cache <cfile> : Enable virtual write support.
     <cfile> specifies the cache file to use.
   --in <itype> <ifile> : Input image format and source file(s). May be specified multiple times.
     <itype> can be "qcow", "qcow2", "qemu", "aff", "aff3", "raw", "dd", "aaff", "vdi", "aewf", "ewf".
     <ifile> specifies the source file. If your image is split into multiple files, you have to specify them all!
   --inopts <iopts> : Specify input library specific options.
     <iopts> specifies a comma separated list of key=value options. See below for details.
   --info : Print out infos about used compiler and libraries.
   --morph <mtype> : Morphing function to apply to input image(s). If not specified, defaults to "combine".
     <mtype> can be "unallocated", "raid0", "combine".
   --morphopts <mopts> : Specify morphing library specific options.
     <mopts> specifies a comma separated list of key=value options. See below for details.
   --offset <off> : Move the output image data start <off> bytes into the input image(s).
   --out <otype> : Output image format. If not specified, defaults to "raw".
     <otype> can be "raw", "dmg", "vdi", "vhd", "vmdk", "vmdks".
   --owcache <file> : Same as --cache <file> but overwrites existing cache file.
   --rocache <file> : Same as --cache <file> but does **not** allow further writes.
   --sizelimit <size> : The data end of input image(s) is set to no more than <size> bytes after the data start.
   --version : Same as --info.
 
 mntp:
   Mount point where output image should be located.
 
 Infos:
 * One --in option and a mount point are mandatory!
 * If you specify --in multiple times, data from all images is morphed into one output image using the specified morphing function.
 * For VMDK emulation, you have to uncomment "user_allow_other" in /etc/fuse.conf or run xmount as root.
 
 Input / Morphing library specific options:
 Input / Morphing libraries might support an own set of options to configure / tune their behaviour.
 Libraries supporting this feature (if any) and their options are listed below.
 
 - libxmount_input_aaff.so
   aaffmaxmem   : Maximum amount of RAM cache, in MiB, for image seek offsets. Default: 10 MiB
   aafflog      : Log file name.
   Specify full path for aafflog. The given file name is extended by _<pid>.
   The AFF format has been declared as deprecated by its inventor!
 
 - libxmount_input_vdi.so
   vdilog       : Path for writing log file(must exist).
                  The files created in this directory will be named log_<pid>.
 
 - libxmount_input_aewf.so
   aewfmaxmem   : Maximum amount of RAM cache, in MiB, for image offset tables. Default: 10 MiB
   aewfmaxfiles : Maximum number of concurrently opened image segment files. Default: 10
   aewfstats    : Output statistics at regular intervals to this directory (must exist).
                  The files created in this directory will be named stats_<pid>.
   aewfrefresh  : The update interval, in seconds, for the statistics (aewfstats must be set). Default: 10s.
   aewflog      : Path for writing log file (must exist).
                  The files created in this directory will be named log_<pid>.
   aewfthreads  : Max. number of threads for parallelized decompression. Default: System CPUs (6)
                  A value of 1 switches back to old, single-threaded legacy functions.
 
 - libxmount_morphing_unallocated.so
   unallocated_fs : Specify the filesystem to extract unallocated blocks from. Supported filesystems are: 'hfs', 'fat'. Default: autodetect.
 
 - libxmount_morphing_raid.so
   raid_chunksize : Specify the chunk size to use in bytes. Defaults to 524288 (512k).

examples.command: xmount v1.2.1 Copyright (c) 2024-2025 by SITS Sarl <development@sits.lu>

local_help.--help:
ERROR: ParseCmdLine@729 : Unknown command line option "--help"

xmount v1.2.1 Copyright (c) 2024-2025 by SITS Sarl <development@sits.lu>

Usage:
  xmount [fopts] <xopts> <mntp>

Options:
  fopts:
    -d : Enable FUSE's and xmount's debug mode.
    -h : Display this help message.
    -s : Run single threaded.
    -o no_allow_other : Disable automatic addition of FUSE's allow_other option.
    -o <fopts> : Specify fuse mount options. Will also disable automatic addition of FUSE's allow_other option!

  xopts:
    --cache <cfile> : Enable virtual write support.
      <cfile> specifies the cache file to use.
    --in <itype> <ifile> : Input image format and source file(s). May be specified multiple times.
      <itype> can be "vdi", "aaff", "qcow", "qcow2", "qemu", "aff", "aff3", "ewf", "aewf", "raw", "dd".
      <ifile> specifies the source file. If your image is split into multiple files, you have to specify them all!
    --inopts <iopts> : Specify input library specific options.
      <iopts> specifies a comma separated list of key=value options. See below for details.
    --info : Print out infos about used compiler and libraries.
    --morph <mtype> : Morphing function to apply to input image(s). If not specified, defaults to "combine".
      <mtype> can be "unallocated", "combine", "raid0".
    --morphopts <mopts> : Specify morphing library specific options.
      <mopts> specifies a comma separated list of key=value options. See below for details.
    --offset <off> : Move the output image data start <off> bytes into the input image(s).
    --out <otype> : Output image format. If not specified, defaults to "raw".
      <otype> can be "raw", "dmg", "vdi", "vhd", "vmdk", "vmdks".
    --owcache <file> : Same as --cache <file> but overwrites existing cache file.
    --rocache <file> : Same as --cache <file> but does **not** allow further writes.
    --sizelimit <size> : The data end of input image(s) is set to no more than <size> bytes after the data start.
    --version : Same as --info.

  mntp:
    Mount point where output image should be located.

Infos:
  * One --in option and a mount point are mandatory!
  * If you specify --in multiple times, data from all images is morphed into one output image using the specified morphing function.
  * For VMDK emulation, you have to uncomment "user_allow_other" in /etc/fuse.conf or run xmount as root.

Input / Morphing library specific options:
  Input / Morphing libraries might support an own set of options to configure / tune their behaviour.
  Libraries supporting this feature (if any) and their options are listed below.

  - libxmount_input_vdi.so
    vdilog       : Path for writing log file(must exist).
                   The files created in this directory will be named log_<pid>.

  - libxmount_input_aaff.so
    aaffmaxmem   : Maximum amount of RAM cache, in MiB, for image seek offsets. Default: 10 MiB
    aafflog      : Log file name.
    Specify full path for aafflog. The given file name is extended by _<pid>.
    The AFF format has been declared as deprecated by its inventor!

  - libxmount_input_aewf.so
    aewfmaxmem   : Maximum amount of RAM cache, in MiB, for image offset tables. Default: 10 MiB
    aewfmaxfiles : Maximum number of concurrently opened image segment files. Default: 10
    aewfstats    : Output statistics at regular intervals to this directory (must exist).
                   The files created in this directory will be named stats_<pid>.
    aewfrefresh  : The update interval, in seconds, for the statistics (aewfstats must be set). Default: 10s.
    aewflog      : Path for writing log file (must exist).
                   The files created in this directory will be named log_<pid>.
    aewfthreads  : Max. number of threads for parallelized decompression. Default: System CPUs (16)
                   A value of 1 switches back to old, single-threaded legacy functions.

  - libxmount_morphing_unallocated.so
    unallocated_fs : Specify the filesystem to extract unallocated blocks from. Supported filesystems are: 'hfs', 'fat'. Default: autodetect.

  - libxmount_morphing_raid.so
    raid_chunksize : Specify the chunk size to use in bytes. Defaults to 524288 (512k).

local_help.man:
xmount(1)                            xmount                           xmount(1)

NAME
     xmount - Tool to crossmount between multiple input and output harddisk im-
     age files

SYNOPSIS
     xmount [fopts] <xopts> <mntp>

DESCRIPTION
     xmount  allows you to convert on-the-fly between multiple input and output
     harddisk image formats. xmount creates a virtual file  system  using  FUSE
     (Filesystem  in  Userspace)  that contains a virtual representation of the
     input image.

     The virtual representation can be in raw DD,  Apple's  Disk  Image  format
     (DMG),  VirtualBox's  virtual  disk file format (VDI), Microsoft's Virtual
     Hard Disk Image format (VHD) or in VmWare's VMDK file format.

     Input images can be raw DD, EWF (Expert Witness Compression  Format),  AFF
     (Advanced  Forensic  Format  v3  & possibly v4 (check xmount --info)), VDI
     (VirtualBox Virtual Disk Image) or QCOW (QEMU Copy On Write) files.

     It is also possible to apply morphing functions to input images.  You  can
     for example combine multiple input images into one output image, or recon-
     struct raids on-the-fly.

     And finally, xmount also supports virtual write access to the output files
     that  is  redirected  to  a cache file. This makes it possible to boot ac-
     quired harddisk images using QEMU, KVM, VirtualBox, VmWare or alike.

OPTIONS
     fopts: (Options specific to FUSE)
       -d: Enable FUSE's and xmount's debug mode.
       -h: Display this help message.
       -s: Run single threaded.
       -o no_allow_other: Disable automatic addition of FUSE's allow_other  op-
     tion.
       -o <fmopts> :
         Specify  fuse  mount  options. Will also disable automatic addition of
     FUSE's
         allow_other option!

     xopts: (Options specific to xmount)
       --cache <cfile> : Enable virtual write support.
         <cfile> specifies the cache file to use.
       --in <itype> <ifile> : Input image format and  source  file(s).  May  be
     specified multiple times.
         For  a list of supported <itype> types, run xmount --info and look un-
     der "loaded input libraries".
         <ifile> specifies the source file. If your image is split into  multi-
     ple files, you have to specify them all!
       --inopts <iopts> : Specify input library specific options.
         <iopts> specifies a comma separated list of key=value options.
       --info : Print out infos about used compiler and loaded libraries.
       --morph  <mtype>  : Morphing function to apply to input image(s). If not
     specified, defaults to "combine".
         For a list of supported <mtype> types, run xmount --info and look  un-
     der "loaded morphing libraries".
       --morphopts <mopts> : Specify morphing library specific options.
         <mopts> specifies a comma separated list of key=value options.
       --offset  <off>  : Move the output image data start <off> bytes into the
     input image(s).
       --out <otype> : Output image  format.  If  not  specified,  defaults  to
     "raw".
         <otype> can be "raw", "dmg", "vdi", "vhd", "vmdk", "vmdks".
       --owcache  <file> : Same as --cache <file> but overwrites existing cache
     file.
       --rocache <file> : Same as --cache <file> but does **not** allow further
     writes.
       --sizelimit <size> : The data end of input image(s) is set  to  no  more
     than <size> bytes after the data start.
       --version : Same as --info.

     mntp:
       Mount point where virtual files should be located.

BUGS
     Hopefully none. If you find any, please e-mail to <bugs@sits.lu>.

EXAMPLE
     To  xmount  an  EWF  image from your acquired disk as a raw DD image under
     /mnt, use the following command:

       xmount --in aewf ./acquired_disk.E?? /mnt

     To xmount the same ewf image as vdi file, you would  use  a  command  like
     this:

       xmount --in aewf ./acquired_disk.E?? --out vdi /mnt

     And  to  enable  virtual write support on a raw DD input image xmounted as
     VDI file:

       xmount --in raw ./acquired_disk.dd --out vdi --cache ./disk.cache /mnt

Daniel Gillen                     Mar 15, 2024                        xmount(1)
local_help.apropos: xmount (1)           - Tool to crossmount between multiple input and output h...

apt_metadata.description:
tool for crossmounting between disk image formats
xmount allows you to convert on-the-fly between multiple input and
output harddisk image formats. xmount creates a virtual file system
using FUSE (Filesystem in Userspace) that contains a virtual
representation of the input image.
.
The virtual representation can be in raw DD, Apple's Disk Image format
(DMG), VirtualBox's virtual disk file format (VDI), Microsoft's Virtual
Hard Disk Image format (VHD) or in VMware's VMDK file format.
.
Input images can be raw DD, EWF (Expert Witness Compression Format),
AFF (Advanced Forensic Format), VDI (VirtualBox Virtual Disk Image) or
QCOW (QEMU Copy On Write) files.
.
In addition, xmount also supports virtual write access to the output
files that is redirected to a cache file. This makes it for example
possible to boot acquired harddisk images using QEMU, KVM, VirtualBox,
VWware or alike.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use xmount because xmount - Tool to crossmount between multiple input and output harddisk image files.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `xmount [fopts] <xopts> <mntp>` as documented upstream for xmount.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `xmount --in aewf ./acquired_disk.E?? /mnt` as documented upstream for xmount.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `xmount --in aewf ./acquired_disk.E?? --out vdi /mnt` as documented upstream for xmount.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `xmount --in raw ./acquired_disk.dd --out vdi --cache ./disk.cache /mnt` as documented upstream for xmount.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair xmount with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `xmount --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.readme: xmount - Tool to crossmount between multiple input and output harddisk image files
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install xmount. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `xmount [fopts] <xopts> <mntp>`. Further invocations from upstream: `xmount --in aewf ./acquired_disk.E?? /mnt`; `xmount --in aewf ./acquired_disk.E?? --out vdi /mnt`. xmount - Tool to crossmount between multiple input and output harddisk image files.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: xmount - Tool to crossmount between multiple input and output harddisk image files.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: xmount - Tool to crossmount between multiple input and output harddisk image files.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install xmount
